name: hydroponic

environment_notes.mars: Robust and familiar, but less water efficient under severe scarcity.
environment_notes.moon: Good baseline choice for stable operations with simpler upkeep.
environment_notes.iss: Well suited to controlled, crew-accessible growth modules.

notes: Simple and proven system with moderate efficiency and lower operational overhead.

name: aeroponic

environment_notes.mars: Strong fit when water is scarce and complexity can be tolerated.
environment_notes.moon: Useful for water-limited missions, but demands tighter monitoring.
environment_notes.iss: Efficient but operationally heavier for a compact station crew.

notes: Highest water efficiency, but also the most complex and energy-intensive option.

name: hybrid

environment_notes.mars: Balanced compromise between resilience, efficiency, and flexibility.
environment_notes.moon: Useful middle-ground system for mixed mission priorities.
environment_notes.iss: Balanced option when crew time and system redundancy both matter.

notes: Balanced system that trades some efficiency for resilience and adaptability.
